title: lawrencia cinerea
intro: Lawrencia cinerea, also known as Grey Midge, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its greyish-green foliage and small white or pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lawrencia cinerea include relief from congestion, sore throats, and bronchitis, making it a popular herbal remedy among Indigenous Australians. Lawrencia cinerea is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
